mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean returns following rate cuts over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both grow, with a Rank IC below 0.02 across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during inflationary periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions fail to predict negative 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 4.0% establishes a restrictive monetary stance that curbs speculative leverage and reduces equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as volatility contracts.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% over a 4-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market deterioration rather than cyclical friction, which suppresses corporate revenue growth and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields a positive 60-day return in over 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions propagate through equity markets.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC below 0.01 across a 10-year rolling window, or returns are negative in majority of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production exceeding 0.5% reflects immediate supply chain constraints and reduced factory utilization, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when credit spreads are widening.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines > 0.5% fail to predict negative 5-day returns when credit spreads widen, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 40% over a 3-year period.
